answer_key.explanation: x = 6. Subtract 5 from both sides to get 2x = 12; divide both sides by 2 to get x = 6. Linear equations always solve by isolating the variable, undoing the operations in reverse order.
common_mistakes: Students often divide the right-hand side by 2 first; this leads to 8.5 instead of 6 because they apply division before subtraction.
